title: The Slavic Creation: Rod and the World Egg
description: The pre-Christian Slavic creation myth: the supreme god Rod hatches the cosmos from a golden egg.
introduction: Pre-Christian Slavic mythology was systematically destroyed after Christianization, but fragments survive in folklore, medieval polemics against paganism, and comparative reconstruction. The supreme deity Rod (whose name means 'birth' or 'clan') was said to have created the cosmos from a golden egg — a motif shared with Hindu, Egyptian, and Finnish creation myths. The dualism of Belobog (White God) and Chernobog (Black God) structured Slavic cosmology.

translation.original_text: Въ начале бысть тъкмо Родъ, а инъ ничтоже. И изъ яйца златаго изыде свет и тьма.
translation.text: In the beginning there was only Rod, and nothing else. And from the golden egg came light and darkness.

translation.original_text: И раздели Родъ свет от тьмы. Свет — то Белобогъ. Тьма — то Чернобогъ.
translation.text: And Rod divided light from darkness. The light was Belobog. The darkness was Chernobog.
